column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline scan rule overrides.
# Format: RULE_ID
# Action: IGNORE | WARN | FAIL
# Strategy:
#  * Rules that depend on HTTPS / non-localhost hostname → IGNORE on http://localhost.*
#    (browsers reject HSTS / Secure-cookie attributes over HTTP-localhost anyway, so
#    flagging them on the loopback target is a false positive for a real production deploy).
#  * Rules that catch genuine misconfiguration regardless of host → FAIL.
#  * Rules that report on a header SET on every response by the gateway / module hosts →
#    not present here because they pass naturally now.
# 10035 Strict-Transport-Security: off on localhost (browsers won't honor it). Production
# scan against a real https host catches this.
# 10038 CSP Header Not Set: gateway sets a CSP for catch-all + SPA HTML; modules set a tighter
# `default-src 'none'` for JSON responses. Both layers covered → FAIL on production hosts to
# regress-protect.
# 10054 Cookie Without Secure Flag: BFF cookie defaults to `SecurePolicy = SameAsRequest`,
# so over HTTP-localhost it correctly drops the Secure flag. Over HTTPS in production it
# adds it. Suppress on localhost; FAIL on real hosts.
# 10055 CSP: Wildcard Directive. The DEV gateway CSP intentionally relaxes script-src to
# 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' for Vite HMR (and the gateway root launchpad uses an inline
# <style>), which ZAP flags as a permissive directive. The ZAP baseline only ever scans the
# dev stack (http://localhost), and the production CSP is strict (script-src 'self', no
# unsafe-eval), so this is IGNORE.
# NOTE: zap-baseline.py's -c config treats the 3rd column as free-text, NOT a URL regex, and
# the last action for a rule id wins — so a single IGNORE line is required (two lines with
# IGNORE then FAIL would resolve to FAIL).

column3: dev-only CSP relaxation for Vite; prod CSP is strict

column1: # 90004 Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy Header Missing. The gateway deliberately sets COOP + CORP
# but not COEP — require-corp/credentialless cross-origin isolation needs every subresource to
# opt in and is not part of the chosen baseline; it can break the SPAs. This is a hardening
# WARN, not a vulnerability, and `fail_action: true` fails the build on warnings too, so IGNORE.
# 10094 Base Tag Analysis: the SPA's index.html has `<base href="/">`; that's expected.
# 10202 Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens: irrelevant on a Bearer-JWT API. Always IGNORE on the
# API surface; FAIL on any HTML page returned by the gateway (the SPA shell — but the SPA
# is signed-in via the BFF cookie + access token, not via state-changing form POSTs from
# arbitrary origins).
# 10010 Cookie No HttpOnly Flag: BFF auth cookie is HttpOnly via the cookie handler default;
# if a future regression flips it off this rule catches it.
# 10037 Server Header Leaks: gateway / Kestrel strip `Server` header by default in Production;
# fail if it ever leaks (information disclosure).
# 10030 Cookie Loosely Scoped to Parent Domain: cookies should scope to the gateway origin
# only, not a parent suffix.
# 10020 X-Frame-Options Header: gateway + modules always set DENY. Catch regressions.
# 10021 X-Content-Type-Options Header: gateway + modules always set nosniff. Catch regressions.
# 10027 Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments: PII-bearing comments in HTML responses
# would be a clinical compliance issue. FAIL.
# 10037 Information Disclosure - Server Banner / .NET Version: don't leak runtime version.
# Rules that fire on the SPA HTML (sourcemap reference, comments). The SPA in CI ships
# unminified — sourcemaps are fine in dev. Mark WARN, not FAIL.
# 10049 Storable and Cacheable Content: APIs return Cache-Control: no-store via the module
# pipeline; the SPA bundle is cacheable (versioned filenames). Both shapes are correct.
